title: Crepidomanes ramitrichum
intro: Crepidomanes ramitrichum, also known as the thymus-like fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes ramitrichum include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Crepidomanes ramitrichum is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Crepidomanes ramitrichum is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is a part of the order Hymenophyllales. The Hymenophyllales order is a subgroup of the division Pteridophyta. Pteridophyta is a division of vascular plants, which also includes ferns and their allies. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Crepidomanes ramitrichum.

Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Crepidomanes ramitrichum.
